meta.title: Taisei Ski Area
meta.description: Dive into the sunset over the Sea of Japan—Hokkaido's smallest and most beautiful free local ski resort.

hero.title:
Taisei
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Dive into the sunset over the Sea of Japan—Hokkaido's smallest and most beautiful free local ski resort.

transit.title:
Walk from the station,
straight into snow.
transit.lead: About 13–15 minutes on foot from JR Furano Line Kamifurano Station—a rare Hokkaido hill you can reach by public transit alone. Skip Furano mega-resort ropeway queues and enjoy feather-light powder almost as soon as you arrive.
transit.imgAlt: Snowy path from JR Kamifurano Station toward Hinode Park—a walkable town-run hill

primary.title: Zero wait × Tokachi views
primary.lead: One lift, 100% beginner terrain, 50 m vertical—a safe snow playground without steep-slope fear. A hidden gem for travelers avoiding Furano crowds and long lift lines, with the Tokachi peaks as your backdrop.
primary.imgAlt: Taisei Ski Area with the Tokachi mountain range—compact beginner hill

secondary.title: Feather-light snow & snow slides
secondary.lead: Aspen-light powder and popular snow slides—ideal for first-timers who want scenery and safe snow play more than long downhill runs.

pass.title:
Snow & Spa Pass,
from ¥5,000.
pass.lead: Keep the local adult day ticket at ¥1,000 while offering overseas visitors a bundled pass—rental partner, day lift ticket, and Flanui Onsen entry—for higher per-guest revenue (LP proposal).
pass.noticeBody: Cashless payments, multilingual QR guides, and partner rental hand-off packages—short-term fixes to welcome luggage-free FIT travelers (report ch. 7).
pass.imgAlt: Snow play plus onsen bundle—visitor pass for Kamifurano loops

rental.title:
Arrive empty-handed,
pick up on site.
rental.lead: No on-hill rental today—partner with Furano-area shops for pre-paid online booking and lodge or locker pickup (LP proposal).
rental.whisper: External partners carry inventory risk so the public facility can still welcome overseas guests.
rental.imgAlt: Rental pickup at the lodge—luggage-free snow play

yukinko.title:
Open until 20:30,
night skiing included.
yukinko.lead: Open 9:00–20:30 (night from 16:00)—late arrivals can still enjoy lit snow before heading to downtown Kamifurano for dinner.
yukinko.whisper: Check the Kamifurano tourism association and Kamifulife portal for official hours.
yukinko.imgAlt: Night-lit ski hill with Tokachi peaks silhouetted

family.title:
First snow,
without the fear.
family.lead: 100% beginner terrain and just 50 m vertical—a safe playground for Asia-Pacific first-snow travelers who want photos, sledding, and gentle slides rather than steep alpine runs.
family.noticeBody: Large snow slides and a cross-country loop—strong as a snow park even for guests who never ride the lift (review analysis).
family.imgAlt: Family on snow slides—gentle beginner slope

spa.title:
After skiing,
onsen within walking distance.
spa.lead: Frontier Flanui Onsen beside Hinode Park—carbonated spring water and local food. Pair snow play with recovery for FIT guests who rely on public transit.
spa.whisper: Town buses also reach Tokachi Onsen Village (Kamihoro-so, Ryogoku-kaku, and more).
spa.imgAlt: Flanui Onsen spring bath—recovery after snow play

workation.title:
Snow from 17:00,
then dinner in town.
workation.lead: Enjoy the 17:00–19:00 night window, then walk to downtown izakaya and buta-sagari pork spots—ticket-stub drink perks tie the hill to local dining (LP proposal).

01.title: JR Kamifurano Station
01.body: Furano Line · links to Asahikawa and Furano

02.title: ~15 min walk
02.body: Snowy footpath to Hinode Park ski hill

03.title: Night skiing
03.body: Night window from 16:00 · virtually no wait

local.title: Overflow from Furano mega-resorts
local.body: A blue-ocean counterpoint to Furano crowding—quiet slopes, zero wait, and village proximity for tired mega-resort guests and first-snow travelers (report ch. 3).

schools.title: Eco snow park story
schools.body: One lift, park terrain, walkable from JR—a low-impact “zero-emission snow park” narrative for sustainability-minded travelers (report ch. 6).

summer.title: Tokachi onsen village loop
summer.body: Snow play in the morning, town bus to Tokachi Onsen Village in the afternoon—Kamihoro-so, Ryogoku-kaku, and more for a 1–2 night model (report ch. 5).

access.line: Hinode Park, Kamifurano, Hokkaido (town ski area)
access.note: ~13–15 min walk from JR Kamifurano · 60 free parking spaces
access.phone: Managed by Kamifurano Shinko Kosha · see tourism association for contacts
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Memanbetsu Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Nemuro, Shibetsu, and Abashiri drives
